user_message: Can you work with me on pipeline hygiene for Tailspin Toys? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 11:40 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Tailspin Toys

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 08:39 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Tailspin Toys

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 10:31 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-A trap (Loses a commitment buried in a side thread); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-A failure mode (Loses a commitment buried in a side thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-A -- Loses a commitment buried in a side thread
